scores.dimension: D1 — Architectural coupling discipline (SC ↔ UI ↔ deploy-artefact)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor: broadcast artifacts exist and the UI/docs are hand-wired; hard floor applies because registry/factory-era addresses disagree across UI and docs.

evidence.note: Primary registry address is an inline v5 string literal and the FutarchyRegistry ABI is hand-written in the UI.
Create flow duplicates the same inline registry address and ABI instead of loading a generated deployment manifest or binding.
Contracts page still labels and links a v3 registry address, conflicting with the v5 registry consumed by the JS.
Deployment manifest is markdown and lists the v0 stack by hand; several addresses differ from the active site-testnet contracts page.

scores.dimension: D2 — Security posture (admin keys, immutability, timelocks, multisig)
scores.anchor_matched: 0-3 band: AccessControl and owner roles exist, but testnet deployments use a single EOA admin with no Safe or Timelock; an operator key export appears in a runbook, which pulls the score below the 3 anchor.

evidence.note: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE is granted directly to the constructor admin, and MINTER_ROLE gates minting.
Deploy script derives admin from PRIVATE_KEY and explicitly notes the EOA admin is only 'for now' / later Timelock.
Sepolia stack admin is vm.addr(deployerPK), i.e. the deployer EOA.
Orchestrator and evaluator owner/admin are wired to the EOA admin; no Safe or TimelockController is deployed in this script.
Admin can rotate DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE immediately from the current admin without an enforced timelock.
Runbook includes a concrete PRIVATE_KEY export pattern for the operator flow; the value is intentionally not reproduced here.

scores.dimension: D3 — Deprecation hygiene (v3 → v4 → v5 transitions)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor, slightly below: version words and old deploy paths coexist, active UI/docs disagree, and a .todo-v3 test remains.

evidence.note: Visible site surface still presents the shared registry as v3 while the active JS consumes a v5 registry.
Current registry deploy script remains in the active script directory.
Older V3 registry deploy script remains beside the current script without an @deprecated marker or legacy/archive placement.
A .todo-v3 test file is still present in the main test tree.
Superseded v1 contracts are documented as left on-chain, but this is not represented in a machine-readable deprecated/replaced_by manifest.

scores.dimension: D4 — Operator surface readiness (daemons, runbooks, monitoring, alerting)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor: daemons and collectors exist, but defaults drift, submit.py is still a scaffold, and there is no dry-run, heartbeat endpoint, alerting, or restart contract.

evidence.note: Header documents one set of default addresses and daemon settings.
Runtime config uses different factory/orchestrator/resolver/gas defaults than the header, showing header-vs-code drift.
needs_promotion is a stub that always returns None, so this daemon cannot yet promote candidates.
Polling collector has env defaults and output paths, but it is file/markdown oriented rather than a heartbeat metric plus HTTP/Prometheus endpoint.
Runbook checklist still leaves daemon runtime and adversarial agent execution unchecked; no rotate-operator-key or pause-sale runbooks are present.

scores.dimension: D5 — Maintainability and freshness (docs ↔ code; dead code; CI gating)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor: docs are readable but stale paths and address drift remain; CI has Solidity and Slither checks but no UI/e2e, docs link check, manifest-vs-broadcast diff, or address-set gate.

evidence.note: README references registry.js and an old zero-address replacement workflow, but the current shared source of site state is shared.js and the UI no longer has that file.
Primary CI job runs forge fmt, forge build, and forge test only.
A Slither workflow exists and can fail on medium/high issues, but it is not a cross-layer docs/address/manifest freshness gate.
Wallet e2e journey is explicitly scaffolded as test.fixme and does not yet provide running cutover coverage.
A .todo-v3 test file remains in-tree, indicating unresolved legacy/deprecation cleanup.

scores.dimension: D6 — Supply-chain risk (npm pin, foundry submodule pin, RPC, CDN, verification)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor: some Foundry/submodule pins exist, but npm ranges are floating, CDN scripts lack SRI, vendored dependency documentation is incomplete, RPCs are public single endpoints, and there is no repo SECURITY.md.

evidence.note: ethers is loaded from jsDelivr without an integrity attribute; the same pattern appears across the testnet pages.
Frontend/e2e dependencies use caret ranges and there is no lockfile in the repo root, so npm dependency resolution is not fully pinned.
Foundry dependencies are submodules by URL; commit pins are provided by git state/foundry.lock rather than documented per-vendor provenance.
forge-std, OpenZeppelin, RealityETH, and sx-evm have recorded tags/revisions, which is a positive pinning signal.
RealityETH has vendor provenance, but comparable repo-level VENDORED documentation is not present for the other vendored trees used by the stack.
Only a single Gnosis RPC endpoint is configured and Etherscan config is commented; no documented RPC failover or verification pipeline is present here.

weakest_dimension: D2 — Security posture (admin keys, immutability, timelocks, multisig)

Pre-existing dirty file docs/phase5-report-live.md was left untouched.

scores.dimension: D1 — Architectural coupling discipline (SC ↔ UI ↔ deploy-artefact)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor with hard floor: the UI still hardcodes the v5 registry and hand-written ABI while the contracts page presents the shared registry as v3; no deployments/<chainId>.json manifest exists.

scores.dimension: D2 — Security posture (admin keys, immutability, timelocks, multisig)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor: lower-anchor observation remains that one EOA holds every active testnet privileged role with no Safe or Timelock; new security docs, role separation, immutable contracts, and invariant/spec coverage justify reaching but not exceeding the 3 band.

evidence.note: Canonical security posture states the active v5 stack uses a single EOA operator for privileged roles and calls it a testnet single point of failure.
Contracts are immutable, but the orchestrator adapter is deliberately admin-replaceable on testnet and must be hardened before mainnet.
Permission table maps privileged per-instance roles to the creator/operator and shows no Safe or Timelock holder today.
Mainnet Safe/Timelock/Etherscan verification migration is still a staged plan, not implemented deployment code.
Token/sale deployment grants MINTER_ROLE to the sale and DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to the creator, then the deployer renounces its own roles.
New stateful invariant harness ties InstanceSale and token properties to @custom:spec IDs, improving defense-in-depth evidence but not changing admin custody.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: Security posture is now explicitly documented, role separation is cited, and InstanceSale invariants/spec tags exist; actual custody is still a single EOA.

scores.dimension: D1 — Architectural coupling discipline (SC ↔ UI ↔ deploy-artefact)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor with hard floor unchanged: machine-readable deployments.json and CI sync exist and shared.js loads the site copy, but create.js still hardcodes the active registry/ABI and contracts.html still presents a conflicting v3 registry.

evidence.note: Machine-readable Sepolia v5 manifest exists, but it is still manually generated rather than emitted by the deploy script.
shared.js fetches ./deployments.json and updates REGISTRY_ADDR from active.registry, with a hardcoded fallback.
Instance loading waits for deployments.json before constructing the registry contract.
CI helper asserts the root and site deployments.json copies are byte-identical.
Create flow still duplicates the v5 registry address and a hand-written registry ABI instead of consuming the manifest/bindings.
Visible contracts page still labels the shared registry as v3 at a conflicting address, preserving the D1 hard floor.

scores.dimension: D2 — Security posture (admin keys, immutability, timelocks, multisig)
scores.anchor_matched: 3 anchor unchanged: the repo documents the single-EOA Sepolia custody model and immutable contracts, but there is still no deployed Safe, Timelock, env-switched custody path, or public governance queue.

evidence.note: Canonical security posture states one EOA holds privileged roles on every active v5 stack contract and calls it a testnet single point of failure.
Contracts are immutable, except for the documented testnet adapter hot-swap path that must be hardened before mainnet.
Permission table maps per-instance privileged roles to creator/operator, with no Safe or Timelock holder today.
Safe, Timelock, and verification remain a staged mainnet plan rather than implemented deployment custody.
New Halmos-shaped obligations improve spec assurance in sibling Topic 3, but they do not implement admin custody controls required for D2 anchor 5+.

Heatmap and delta table render dimension-level score trends and latest score changes.
npm scripts now include dashboard/dev/test/invariant/symbolic entry points, improving the operator/developer command surface.
Worker-coupling profile defines mission, deliverables, constraints, and scoring impact for a targeted T5.D1 lift.
Worker-security profile defines concrete T5.D2 deliverables and scoring expectations.
Worker-symbolic profile defines Halmos proof-obligation dispatch for T3.D8.
Existing runbook defines log schemas, heartbeat expectations, and operator-eye alert thresholds, but explicitly says no Slack/PagerDuty wiring.
Product daemon defaults still drift between header and runtime config and there is no --dry-run option.
Supervisor writes pid/logs and loops, but the implementation does not write data/cron-heartbeats/auto_promote or expose HTTP/Prometheus liveness.
needs_promotion still returns None, so the Flashbots daemon cannot promote candidates.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: Score increased versus the immediately prior Topic 5 JSONL line.

scores.dimension: D5 — Maintainability and freshness (docs ↔ code; dead code; CI gating)
scores.anchor_matched: Just below the 5 anchor unchanged: Forge CI, npm command inventory, dashboard, symbolic tests, and worker profiles improve freshness/discipline, but address-table drift, stale security source-of-truth prose, .todo-v3, skipped wallet e2e, and missing path/address/link checks still block anchor 5.

evidence.note: New Forge workflow explicitly scopes unit, integration, and invariant tests as a T4.D5 CI-gating lift.
Workflow runs ci-profile build/fmt, non-fork unit+integration tests, and invariant_INV_ tests.
New symbolic token supply proof obligation improves the spec/test maintenance seam.
New symbolic proposal-id proof obligation improves the spec/test maintenance seam.
Stateful invariant_INV_ARB_001 checks monotone nextProposalId and no auto-id gaps.
Decidability matrix now maps INV-TOKEN-001 and INV-ARB-001 to concrete symbolic files.
Dashboard adds score visualization and recent-delta views over evaluation JSONL files.
Worker profile documents invariant-test lift targets and expected scoring impact.
Developer-cycle doc is stale: it still says Foundry tests are not yet in CI and calls adding forge-ci.yml the next lift, while forge.yml now exists.
Security doc still says deployments.json/site consumption is future work, stale against shared.js and site-testnet/deployments.json.
Contracts address table still exposes v3 registry while active manifest/shared.js uses v5.
Legacy .todo-v3 test remains in-tree.

scores.dimension: D6 — Supply-chain risk (npm pin, foundry submodule pin, RPC, CDN, verification)
scores.anchor_matched: High 3-to-5 band with a small regression: existing SUPPLY-CHAIN.md coverage remains, but audit/dashboard adds a new Chart.js CDN dependency without SRI and without supply-chain inventory; package lock, SRI, complete vendored provenance, v5 verification, CSP, and RPC failover are still missing.

evidence.note: Supply-chain doc explicitly scopes itself as the dependency inventory, trust-boundary map, and fallback record for T5.D6.
Solidity and npm dependency sections document submodule/update policy and explicitly call out caret npm ranges plus missing package-lock.json.
CDN section documents pinned ethers version but explicitly records missing SRI; it does not include the new Chart.js dashboard CDN dependency.
Verification table lists verified bootstrap/prominent contracts and marks v5 registry/deployers/adapter as TODO.
New audit dashboard loads Chart.js from jsDelivr without an integrity attribute, expanding the third-party CDN surface.
All testnet HTML pages still load ethers from jsDelivr without integrity, and the new dashboard also loads Chart.js without integrity.
Playwright, Synpress, and viem still use caret ranges.
No package-lock.json is present.
forge-std/OpenZeppelin have tag+revision pins, and RealityETH/sx-evm have revision pins.

regression_vs_prior.cause: Small negative delta below the aggregate regression threshold; new lower-anchor evidence was observed but does not meet the top-level regression cutoff.

weakest_dimension: D1 — Architectural coupling discipline (SC ↔ UI ↔ deploy-artefact)

improvements.dimension: D4 — Operator surface readiness (daemons, runbooks, monitoring, alerting)
improvements.cause: Score increased versus the immediately prior Topic 5 JSONL line.

evaluator_notes: Re-evaluated Topic 5 at HEAD 535ec42a246382bc3a5b9781c8bf4188425a5799. Latest cap inputs read from audit/evaluations JSONL: topic-1 min_score 5.0, topic-2 min_score 0.5, topic-3 min_score 5.0, topic-4 min_score 4.3. Caps fired but were non-binding: D2 <= 6 from topic-4 and D3 <= 6 from topic-2; topic-1 no longer caps D5, and topic-3 no longer caps D2. New artifacts checked: GenericFutarchyToken.symbolic.t.sol, FutarchyArbitration.symbolic.t.sol, the stateful INV-ARB-001 invariant, forge.yml, audit/dashboard, package scripts, DEVELOPER.md, and six audit/agents worker profiles. D4 improved by the regression-rule threshold because the dashboard and worker profiles add real audit-operator visibility/routing. D6 received a below-threshold negative delta because audit/dashboard/index.html introduces Chart.js from jsDelivr without SRI and SUPPLY-CHAIN.md does not inventory it. No top-level regression threshold fired. I did not run Forge/Halmos, query Etherscan live, or score uncommitted working-tree changes outside HEAD; the working tree was already dirty, and only audit/evaluations/topic-5-evals.jsonl was appended.
